close_reason: Fixed: param_keys() was added in commit 88c09a8
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1198-1200: SecretUri::param(&self, key) is the only public read accessor for params. There is no .params() iterator, no .param_keys(), no .has_param().

Maintainer cost: every backend that wants to reject unknown query params — which is the safe default for a security-sensitive URI parser — has to either (a) not check (silent typos, e.g. 'feild=password' becomes a no-op), or (b) work around the missing API by storing the URI string and re-parsing it themselves. The aws-sm, azure-kv, vault, etc. backends will each invent their own approach. That's a class of inconsistency this crate's API can prevent up front.

The doc comment (lib.rs:1093-1095) says 'preserves the ability to change the internal representation'. That's a fine reason to hide the field, but it's not a reason to omit a public iteration accessor — &dyn Iterator<Item=(&str, &str)> or impl Iterator<...> would preserve the same flexibility.

Fix: add SecretUri::params() -> impl Iterator<Item=(&str, &str)> + '_, plus SecretUri::has_param(key) -> bool as a polish accessor.
title: SecretUri exposes params only by key lookup, no iteration or unknown-key detection
